column1: 合理的（な） (ごうりてきな)
強力（な） (きょうりょくな)

column2:
Emphatic impossibility; "it's absolutely impossible."
Connection: Vます(stem) + っこない
Ex: 一日でこの本の文法全部なんて、覚えられっこない。(絶対覚えられない) — It's impossible to memorize all the grammar in this book in one day.
今の実力では、試験に受かりっこない。(絶対に受からない) — It is impossible to pass the test with my current ability.

High possibility of something bad happening.
Connection: Vます(stem) + かねない
Ex: この問題を解決せずに放っておいたら、国際問題になりかねない。(なるかもしれない) — If this problem is left unresolved, it could become an international issue.
そんなひどいことも、あの人なら言いかねない。(言うかもしれない) — If it's him, he might say something that terrible.

Hard to do something or refusal to do something due to hesitation/inability (formal).
Connection: Vます(stem) + かねる
Ex: 申し訳ありませんが、私にはわかりかねます。(わかりません) — I am sorry, but I cannot answer that.
そのようなご依頼は、お引き受けしかねます。(できません) — I cannot accept that request.

Hard to do / hard to feel something.
Connection: Vます(stem) + がたい
Ex: これは信じがたい話だが、事実である。(信じられない) — This is hard to believe, but it is true.
人が人の命をうばうなんて、許しがたい。(許せない) — It is unforgivable that a person would take another's life.

column1: 〜ことから (because / from the fact that)
column2:
Because of X, Y happens (often used for reasons/origins).
Connection: V/A/na/N + ことから (naな/である, Nだ/である)
Ex: 富士山が見えることから、この町は富士見町という名前がついた。(見えるという理由で) — Because Mt. Fuji is visible, this town is named Fujimicho.
桜の名所であることから、春には花見客が大勢やってくる。(名所だという理由で) — Because this is a famous place for cherry blossoms, many people visit in spring.

column1: 〜ことだから (knowing that / since it is X)
column2:
Because of the nature/character of X, Y is expected.
Connection: N + のことだから
Ex: いつも遅刻する彼のことだから、もうすぐ現れるだろう。(彼はいつも〜だから) — Knowing him, since he's always late, he should be here soon.
親切な林さんのことだから、頼めば手伝ってくれるよ。(林さんは親切だから) — Knowing kind Hayashi-san, if you ask, he will help.

Doing something without performing action X (formal).
Connection: Vる + ことなく
Ex: 雨は休むことなく降り続いた。(休まないで) — The rain continued without stopping.
時は止まることなく流れる。(止まらないで) — Time flows without stopping.

If action X is not performed, result Y cannot happen.
Connection: Vない + ことには + Vない
Ex: やってみないことには、できるかどうかわからない。(やってみなければ) — I don't know if I can do it unless I try.
実物を見ないことには、買う気にはなれない。(見なければ) — I don't feel like buying it until I see it.

column1: 〜(な)ら当然だ / 当たり前だ (it is natural that...)
column2:
It is a matter of course.
Connection: V/A/na/N + 当然だ / 当たり前だ
Ex: ひどいことばかり言ったので、彼女に嫌われて当然だ。(普通は嫌われる) — Since you said so many nasty things, it's natural she hates you.
相手のチームは弱い。勝って当たり前だ。(普通は勝つ) — The other team is weak. It's only natural to win.

column1: 〜もの / もっともだ (it is natural / reasonable)
column2:
It is understandable/reasonable.
Connection: V/A/na/N + もっともだ
Ex: 君が裏切ったのだから、彼女が怒るのももっともだ。(普通は怒る) — Since you betrayed her, it's natural she is angry.
あなたがそう言うのはもっともだ。(普通はそう言う) — It is natural for you to say that.

Almost exactly the same / effectively the same.
Connection: V/A/na/N + 同然だ
Ex: この車は中古車といっても新品(も)同然だ。(新品と同じようだ) — Although this is a used car, it is virtually new.
彼の財産はないも同然だ。(ないと言ってもいいくらいだ) — He virtually has no assets.

column1: 〜だけましだ (better than nothing / at least)
column2:
At least I have X (it could be worse).
Connection: V/A/na + だけましだ
Ex: 給料が減ったけれど、首にならないだけましだ。(首にならないからまだいい) — My salary decreased, but at least I didn't get fired.
狭くて高いけれど、便利なだけましだ。(便利だからまだいい) — It's small and expensive, but at least it's convenient.

Since X is the case, Y (the expected outcome) happens even more strongly.
Connection: V/A/na/N + だけに
Ex: 一流ホテルだけあって快適だった。(らしく) — As expected of a first-rate hotel, it was comfortable.
このバッグは安いだけにすぐに壊れてしまった。(安いものらしく) — Just as you'd expect from a cheap bag, it broke immediately.

column1: 〜ばかりか / 〜ばかりでなく (not only... but also...)
column2:
Not only X, but also Y.
Connection: V/A/na/N + ばかりか / ばかりでなく
Ex: あの人は学校の成績がいいばかりかスポーツもできる。 — He is not only good at his school work, but also sports.
このあたりは、空気ばかりでなく水も汚染されている。 — In this area, not only is the air polluted, but so is the water.

Emphasizes that a negative result occurred specifically because of reason X.
Connection: V/A/na/N + ばかりに
Ex: あの飛行機に乗ったばかりに、彼は死んだ。(乗ったために) — Just because he took that plane, he died.
英語の先生が嫌いなばかりに、英語も嫌いになってしまった。(嫌いなために) — Because I disliked the English teacher, I grew to dislike English.

Formal expression for "not only / as well as."
Connection: V/A/na/N + のみならず
Ex: その映画は日本のみならず、外国でもよく知られている。 — The film is well known not only in Japan but also overseas.
料理は味が良いのみならず、見た目も美しい。 — The food is not only delicious, but also looks beautiful.

column1: 〜ではないか / 〜じゃないか (let's / why don't we)
column2:
A strong invitation or expressing firm will (masculine/formal).
Connection: Vよう + ではないか / じゃないか
Ex: 今日はお祝いだ。みんなで飲もうではないか。 — It's a celebration today. Let's all drink together!
誰もやらないなら、ぼくがやってみようじゃないか。 — If no one else will do it, I'll try it!

There is no method or means to perform an action.
Connection: Vます(stem) + ようがない / ようもない
Ex: 何と言ったらいいのか、言いようがない。 — I don't know how to express it (no words for it).
この作文は日本語がめちゃくちゃで直しようがない。 — This essay's Japanese is such a mess, there is no way to fix it.

It looks exactly like something else.
Connection: V/A/na/N + かのようだ / かのように
Ex: まるで空が泣いているかのようだ。 — It is as if the sky is crying.
怖いものでも見たかのように、彼女は震えていた。 — She was shivering as if she had seen something frightening.

column1: 〜そうにない / 〜そうもない (not likely to)
column2:
Little to no possibility of an event happening.
Connection: Vます(stem) + そうにない / そうもない
Ex: 最近仕事がすごく忙しくて、同窓会に行けそうにないよ。 — I'm so busy at work, I don't think I can go to the reunion.
できそうもないことを、できると言ってしまって後悔している。 — I regret saying I could do something that I probably couldn't.

Formal way to say "when doing X" or "at the time of X."
Connection: Vる / N + に際して / に際し / に際しては
Ex: お申し込みに際しては、写真が必要となります。 — A photograph is required when applying.
A氏は日本を訪問するに際し、喜びを語った。 — Mr. A expressed his joy upon visiting Japan.

Based on a rule, plan, or experience.
Connection: N + に基づいて / に基づき / に基づく / に基づいた
Ex: この工事は市の計画に基づいて進められます。 — This construction is proceeding based on the city's plan.
長年の経験に基づき新入社員を教育する。 — I train new employees based on my many years of experience.

column1: 〜に応じて (in response to / depending on)
column2:
Changing or acting in response to specific needs or conditions.
Connection: N + に応じて / に応じ / に応じた
Ex: ソフトは、必要に応じてダウンロードしてください。 — Please download the software as necessary.
テスト結果に基づき、能力に応じたクラスに分けられます。 — Based on your test results, you will be assigned to a class that matches your ability.

Under the influence or guidance of someone/something.
Connection: N + の下で / の下に
Ex: 子どもたちが青空の下で元気に遊んでいる。 — Children are playing cheerfully under the blue sky.
田中先生のご指導の下、研究論文を書いています。 — I am writing my research paper under the guidance of Mr. Tanaka.
a postponement — pushing an event or deadline back to a later date
a worsening of revenue/profits (a company's earnings getting worse)
the opening of a new road or rail line to traffic/use
a criminal trial conducted under Japan's lay judge (citizen juror) system
arson at one's own home (deliberately setting fire to your own house)
a prison sentence that includes compulsory labor
a seasonal sales battle/competition among stores (e.g. the mid-year gift season)
the mainstay/leading focus (e.g. a store's flagship product line)

column1: 財布のひもをゆるめる
column2: to loosen the purse strings — become more willing to spend money
to be considerate of / take (something, e.g. the environment) into account
to stage or produce an effect (deliberately create a certain impression)
a featured/loss-leader product used to draw customers in
possession (of something, e.g. a weapon or drugs)
transfer (of ownership — handing something over to someone else)
suspicion (of a crime, as in a criminal suspect)
is admitting (to something, e.g. a suspect admitting guilt)
a hit-and-run (a traffic accident where the driver flees the scene)
reporting an incident to the police/authorities
an added/extra function on top of a device's basic purpose
to be impaired or spoiled (e.g. a design's appeal being ruined)
to be supported/favored (by a particular group of people)
a pedometer (a device that counts your steps)
to account for / make up (a proportion or percentage of a whole)
to reach / amount to (a certain level or figure)
to exceed / surpass (a figure or expectation)
to fall short of / be below (a figure or expectation)
the source of ignition (what originally caused a fire)
playing with fire (e.g. a child playing with a lighter)
to call oneself / claim to be (something)
sharp, incisive (e.g. sharp social commentary or criticism)
smoothly and fluently (e.g. reading without getting stuck)
